plain_english: Score rises when fresh, concrete escalation signals outweigh stabilization signals.
Score falls when verified de-escalation signals and natural decay dominate.
Confidence rises with higher-quality sources, clearer signals, and less contradiction.
Sparse-data days can flatten movement; this is shown as a limitation, not hidden.
model_mechanics: Pipeline: ingest -> classify -> dedupe -> state update -> coupling -> clamp -> probability mapping.
Signal classes are weighted and then decay-adjusted.
State variables are clamped to [0,10] and probability is bounded by logistic mapping.
Ambiguity penalty reduces confidence when escalation and stabilization signals conflict.
transparency: Source weights are config-driven and shown in descending order.
Back-history policy and anchors are displayed directly.
Build metadata and last update timestamp are shown on page.
Driver list is ranked and limited to avoid duplicate overload.
limitations: Top-line percentage is not a validated real-world probability forecast.
Historical replay quality depends on external data availability and query coverage.
Cross-source event clustering is partial and still under active improvement.
Confidence should be read as model confidence, not truth confidence.
example_scenarios: Escalation example: missile test + troop movement + sanctions pressure -> score up.
Mixed day example: ceasefire talks + fresh shelling -> contradiction penalty, muted movement.
Stabilization example: verified talks + inspection progress + hotline restoration -> score down.
